'''Rann I-Kanu''' was the a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ali-Vor]] and served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
===Early Life===
Rann I-Kanu, a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], was born in [[47 BBY/Legends|47 BBY]]. Soon after, he became a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], accepted for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] and was considered to be strong in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. I-Kanu was later selected as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ali-Vor]], a [[Lore Keeper|Jedi scholar]].

===Battle of Naboo===
At some point prior to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], at the age of 15, Ali-Vor brought I-Kanu to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], furthering the Padawan's education by enrolling him in the [[Royal House of Learning/Legends|Royal House of Learning]] in [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. When Ali-Vor learned that a group of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] were taking local fauna off-world, he tasked I-Kanu with finding out the truth to the rumors. I-Kanu enlisted the help of his friends and classmates; [[Galak]], a young volunteer [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]]; [[Rorworr (Naboo)|Rorworr]], the son of a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]]; and [[Arani Korden]], the adventurous daughter of a [[House Korden]] [[Noble/Legends|nobleman]].

Korden managed to obtain a map to the smuggler's base via one of her contacts, which led the group to the precise location. There, they indeed discovered a hidden base, secreted inside an overgrown jungle shrine, and made their way inside. Once inside, they battled and defeated small group of smugglers, then learned that the group was smuggling dangerous [[Veermok/Legends|veermoks]] off the planet. I-Kanu and the others then discovered [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Tasrah Boh]], a local [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] archeologist from the House of Learning who had been taken captive by the smugglers when he drove his [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] too close to their hideout. After defeating a [[Remote/Legends|Remote]] programmed to attack intruders, they freed Boh from his cage. After conversing with I-Kanu and his friends, Boh fled the hideout.

I-Kanu and the others then encountered [[Saidle Frex]], an [[Armor/Legends|armored]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who hired the smugglers, as well as another man wielding a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|Vibro-ax]]. The Padawan and his friends were able to defeat both men and return to I-Kanu's master with information regarding the smuggling operations. He played a minor role as a member of the [[Naboo Underground]] in defending Naboo from [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] forces during their [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|occupation]] of the planet. Along with his old friends, as well as new allies from the House of Learning, they participated in weapons theft and liberation of prisoners against the [[Droid Army|Droid army]] and the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidians]]. In one particular operation, he freed the Naboo [[Minister of Culture/Legends|Minister of Culture]], [[Kyu Tane]].

===Order 66===
Years later when the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] came to an end, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] ordered the [[The galaxy|galaxy]]-wide execution known as [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], in which [[Clone trooper/Legends|every clone troopers]] were to execute their own [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi Generals]] and [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Commanders]] without question, leading to the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith">{{Film|III}}</ref> After the Republic was transformed into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] under the control of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], Rann I-Kanu was one of the surviving Jedi that avoided the execution of Order 66, but were being pursued by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the apprentice of Emperor Palpatine. Along with [[Sia-Lan Wezz]],<ref name="SWP">[[Purge (comic book)|''Purge'']]</ref> he was declared eliminated by the Sith, while some, including [[Rorworr (Naboo)|Rorworr]], believed he was missing during the Empire's reign across the galaxy.<ref name="FoCC" />

==Powers and abilities==
Among the [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] I-Kanu mastered during his time as a Jedi were [[battlemind]], [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]], and [[Force sight]]. He was also considered to be strong in the Force and an able combatant.
